title: Pupis: Strive
body:
### Want to relax? 
Spend quality time playing Minecraft but lost your drive to play?

## Welcome to Strive
Relive that nostalgic feeling you had when you first launched Minecraft.
That feeling when you broke your first tree, placed your first block, and built your first home.

Welcome back _Home_.

## Why Strive?
Strive is aimed to bring back that love you had way back when, and more.
With mods like [Sodium](https://modrinth.com/mod/sodium) to bring the best performance,
shaders like [Bliss shaders](https://modrinth.com/shader/bliss-shader) to bring the best visuals along with [Distant Horizons](https://modrinth.com/mod/distanthorizons) to relive with nostalgia.
[Create](https://modrinth.com/mod/create) to make anything possible and [Botania](https://modrinth.com/mod/botania) to dabble in the arts.

In Strive, _anything_ is possible


<details>
<summary>Wiki</summary>

# What to see here?
Inside the Wiki you can see some things that may not be that noticable from the get-go, things like **pinging** items, **free cams**, etc!

  ## Recommendations
It's recommended to use at least 8gb RAM

  ## Pinging
You can ping items, entities and locations by using Mouse 5 (this is configurable)

  ## Free Cam
Want to see your builds or explore around you? Press F4 to enable freecam!

</details>



<details>
<summary>Why Pupis?</summary>

Puppis is one of the 88 recognized constellations in the night sky. It represents the stern of a ship and was originally part of a larger constellation called Argo Navis, which symbolized the mythical ship of the Argonauts.
- Location: Puppis is situated in the southern hemisphere and can be observed from latitudes between +40° and -90°.
- Named Stars:
> - Naos (ζ Pup): Also known as “Naos,” this bright star represents the Greek word for “ship.”
> - Tureis (ρ Pup): Its name comes from the Arabic term for “shield.”
> - Asmidiske (ξ Pup): Named after the Greek word for the “gunwale” of a ship.
- Extrasolar Planets: Several planetary systems have been discovered around stars in Puppis, including Neptune-mass planets and even ice giant exoplanets.
</details>
